mcqs.question: Which of the following best defines an operating system?

options.text: A program that manages computer hardware and acts as an intermediary between users and hardware
A collection of application programs for word processing and gaming
The physical components of a computer system
A programming language used to write applications

mcqs.explanation: An operating system is a program that manages computer hardware, provides a basis for application programs, and acts as an intermediary between the computer user and the computer hardware.

mcqs.question: From the system view, the operating system is primarily a:
mcqs.explanation: From the system view, the OS is a resource allocator that manages CPU, memory, I/O devices, and file storage. It is also a control program that manages execution of user programs to prevent errors and improper use of the computer.

mcqs.question: The one program that is always running on the computer is called the:
mcqs.explanation: The kernel is the one program running at all times on the computer. It is the core part of the OS that manages system resources. Everything else is either a system program or an application program.

mcqs.question: In computer system organization, I/O devices inform the CPU they have finished an operation through:
mcqs.explanation: Device controllers inform the CPU that operations are finished by causing an interrupt. The interrupt mechanism is fundamental to how modern computer systems handle I/O efficiently without requiring the CPU to constantly poll devices.

mcqs.question: DMA (Direct Memory Access) is primarily used to:

options.text: Transfer data between device buffers and main memory without CPU intervention

mcqs.explanation: DMA allows device controllers to transfer blocks of data directly to and from main memory without CPU intervention. This frees the CPU from the overhead of byte-by-byte data transfer and only requires one interrupt per block.

mcqs.question: Multiprogramming increases CPU utilization by:

options.text: Organizing jobs so the CPU always has one to execute when the current job waits for I/O
Adding more CPUs to the system
Reducing the number of running programs

mcqs.explanation: Multiprogramming keeps several jobs in memory simultaneously. When the currently executing job must wait for I/O, the OS switches to another job. This ensures the CPU is never idle as long as there are jobs to execute.

mcqs.question: Time-sharing (multitasking) differs from multiprogramming in that:

options.text: The CPU switches between jobs so frequently that users can interact with programs while they run
Only one program runs at a time

mcqs.explanation: Time-sharing extends multiprogramming by switching the CPU between jobs so frequently (using a timer) that each user feels they have their own dedicated computer, enabling interactive use. Multiprogramming only switches on I/O waits.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is the correct order in the storage hierarchy from fastest to slowest?
mcqs.explanation: The storage hierarchy from fastest to slowest is: Registers > Cache > Main Memory > SSD > Magnetic Disk > Optical Disk > Magnetic Tape. Speed decreases and capacity increases as you go down the hierarchy.
